description: Codified ccanvil lifecycle state machine. Consumed by docs-check.sh lifecycle-state to derive legal next actions per current state. See docs/specs/bts-20-lifecycle-state-primitive.md for the design rationale.

states.description: On main with no active spec on the branch. Specs may exist in docs/specs/ backlog. Default starting state.
A spec file exists in docs/specs/ but has not been activated to a branch. Equivalent to no-active-spec when listing scope; this state exists for graph completeness.
On a claude/<type>/<id> feature branch with docs/spec.md present, no plan yet. Lifecycle copied the spec into the active slot via docs-check.sh activate.
Active spec + active plan present on the feature branch. Ready to implement under TDD.
Plan-written state with commits ahead of branch base. Active TDD work in progress.
Feature branch has a PR open (draft or ready). Local detection is best-effort via gh; primarily a logical state.
Transient state — PR was squash-merged on GitHub, local main not yet fast-forwarded.

Session-kind stasis present (regardless of post-compact marker). Session boundary state; legal_next_actions vary based on compact freshness — pre-compact recommends /compact, post-compact recommends forward action.
Validate returned a non-aligned result (stale-plan, stale-stasis, mismatched, unlinked, missing-determinism-review). Recovery action required before further forward motion.
Not a ccanvil project (no .ccanvil/scripts/) or not a git repo. Edge state for substrate primitives invoked from foreign trees.

edges.command: Address validate.details (mismatched / stale-plan / stale-stasis / unlinked / missing-determinism-review)
